reasoning_model_1: Model 1 fails significantly on **Content Accuracy**. It reads the prompt instructions ('Please read this sentence with a very slow pace') included in the audio output (00:00-00:03) before reading the target text. Regarding the **Pace** dimension, the target sentence 'Every word, must be read clearly' is spoken from approximately 00:03 to 00:06. This duration (3 seconds for 6 words) corresponds to a normal conversational rate (~120 wpm), not the 'very slow pace' requested. While the articulation is clear, the model fails to implement the requested paralinguistic feature.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 scores 0 because it fails completely on **Content Accuracy**. Instead of performing the text-to-speech task for the target sentence 'Every word, must be read clearly', the model behaves like a chat assistant, responding with 'Of course, I will read it, slowly' (00:00-00:03) and then ending the audio. Because the audio content is completely inconsistent with the required script (the target sentence is never read), the model receives the lowest possible score as per the global considerations.
model_comparison: Model 1 is the winner, primarily because Model 2 completely failed to perform the reading task. Model 2 provided a conversational acknowledgement instead of reading the target text, resulting in a score of 0 due to empty/incorrect content. Model 1, while flawed, did synthesize the target text. However, Model 1 exhibited major issues: it read the prompt instructions aloud (prompt leakage) and failed to adhere to the 'very slow pace' constraint, delivering the lines at a standard conversational speed. Despite these significant flaws, Model 1 is superior to Model 2 simply because it generated the target text, whereas Model 2 did not.
